BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Back-of-napkin math on what it'd take to rent a small shop someday and do furniture on the side without losing money. Nowhere close yet, just thinking it through.

CurrentState.Note: Right now the whole thing basically breaks even and everything spare goes to the tools fund. No shop rent because I work out of the garage.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: As a garage side gig it already breaks even. The second I take on rent I need steady commissions I don't have yet. Better plan is to keep feeding the tools fund and buy the big tools first, worry about a shop later.
ActionItems: Price the Lie-Nielsen No. 7 and a router table before thinking about rent
Ask around for anyone splitting shop space
